zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret De Houghton於1370年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾霍格頓 堂爾時，她的父親Richard de Houghton28歲，母親Joan De Radcliffe30歲。她於1389年與John Southworth在英國英格蘭藍卡爾薩苗萊斯波利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1424年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾薩苗萊斯波利享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret De Houghton于1370年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾霍格頓 堂爾时，她的父亲Richard de Houghton28岁，母亲Joan De Radcliffe30岁。她于1389年与John Southworth在英国英格兰藍卡爾薩苗萊斯波利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1424年在英国英格兰藍卡爾薩苗萊斯波利享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret De Houghton was born in 1370, in Hoghton Tower, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard de Houghton, was 28 and her mother, Joan De Radcliffe, was 30. She married John Southworth in 1389, in Samlesbury, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1424, in Samlesbury, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret De Houghton est née en 1370, à Hoghton Tower, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Houghton, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan De Radcliffe, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Southworth en 1389, à Samlesbury, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1424, à Samlesbury, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret De Houghtonが1370年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアホートン トウエルで生まれた時，父Richard de Houghtonは28歳，母Joan De Radcliffeは30歳であった。1389年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアサムレスブリーでJohn Southworthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1424年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアサムレスブリーで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret De Houghton(이)가 1370년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 호그h턴 타워에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Houghton(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan De Radcliffe(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1389년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 샘레스버이에서 John Southworth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1424년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 샘레스버이에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret De Houghton родилась в 1370 г. в местности хогтон тоуир, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard de Houghton было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan De Radcliffe, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Southworth в 1389 г. в местности сэмлесбури, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1424 г. в местности сэмлесбури, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет.
